audit.methodology: Static analysis: grep for hardcoded secrets, XSS patterns, unsafe deserialization, SQL injection, credential exposure

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded DeepSeek API key (sk-96c8c...) in config.yaml — tracked in git history. Even though .gitignore now excludes config.yaml, the key is present in all historical commits and must be rotated.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate the DeepSeek API key immediately. Use environment variables with python-dotenv. Add config.yaml to .gitignore (already done). Run git filter-branch or bfg to purge from history, or rotate all keys and force-push.

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded Google API key (AIzaSyBWR...) in config.yaml — committed to git history. Same key exposed in deep_evaluation config section (line 18).
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate the Google API key. Restrict in Google Cloud Console to specific APIs. Use environment variable GOOGLE_API_KEY instead.

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded Alpha Vantage API key (FQZDH795...) in config.yaml — committed to git history.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate the Alpha Vantage API key. Use environment variable ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY.

vulnerabilities.description: Full config snapshot including live API keys (DeepSeek, Google, Alpha Vantage) dumped to effective_config.yaml per pipeline run. 11 such files found. reports/ is NOT in .gitignore — these API keys are at risk of being committed.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add 'reports/' to .gitignore immediately. Redact secrets from effective_config.yaml dumps. Delete existing files containing secrets.

vulnerabilities.description: Another leaked effective config with live API keys in reports directory.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same as above — add 'reports/' to .gitignore and redact or delete secrets from dumped configs.

vulnerabilities.description: Unsafe deserialization: joblib.load() uses pickle, which can execute arbitrary code. Loading untrusted model artifacts is a remote code execution (RCE) vector.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Verify model integrity with cryptographic checksum before loading. Use safer formats (ONNX, safetensors). Restrict to trusted directories only.

vulnerabilities.description: Second unsafe joblib.load() call — same RCE vulnerability via pickle deserialization.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same as above. Apply consistent model-loading security across both call sites.

vulnerabilities.description: External submodule contains .env file with Alpaca trading API credentials. Even if gitignored in the submodule, its presence on disk is a data-exfiltration risk if any pipeline step reads it.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Verify .env is gitignored in the submodule. Audit that no pipeline code reads from external .env files.

vulnerabilities.description: Streamlit dashboard has NO authentication. Anyone with network access can view financial data, trigger pipeline runs, and modify settings. Settings page API key inputs are cosmetic only — they accept input but do not persist or validate.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add streamlit-authenticator or OAuth2 proxy (nginx/oauth2-proxy). At minimum, add server-side password gate using Streamlit secrets.

vulnerabilities.description: Stored XSS: stock metadata (name, exchange, sector, industry) from database rendered via f-string into unsafe_allow_html=True markup. If attacker injects HTML/JS into the stocks table, it executes in admin browsers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: HTML-escape all DB-sourced values before inserting into markdown with unsafe_allow_html. Use st.markdown with default safe mode for user-supplied data.

vulnerabilities.description: Reflected XSS: numeric values (last_close, pct_change) and chg string interpolated into unsafe_allow_html markdown. Currently safe (numeric only) but fragile pattern.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace custom HTML metric cards with st.metric() or ensure all interpolated values are strictly numeric/escaped.

vulnerabilities.description: Reflected XSS: pe_ratio (can be string '—') interpolated into unsafe_allow_html markup. If pe_ratio becomes user-controllable, this is an injection point.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.metric() or escape all values before HTML interpolation.

vulnerabilities.description: 6 API secrets from config.yaml injected into os.environ at runtime. Any child process or library can read them. Visible to error tracebacks and subprocess listings.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Pass API keys explicitly as function arguments. Use context manager to set/restore env vars per backend call instead of polluting global os.environ.

vulnerabilities.description: Same os.environ injection — API keys pushed to global process environment.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same — apply uniform secrets-handling across all backends.

vulnerabilities.description: Third os.environ injection point — database credentials and API keys pushed to global env.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Stop writing secrets to os.environ globally. Use scoped environment or explicit parameter passing.

vulnerabilities.description: reports/ directory NOT in .gitignore. 11 effective_config.yaml files with live API keys exist. One git add . would commit all of them.
13 total unsafe_allow_html sites across 6 files. All current uses are static strings — no active XSS — but the pattern normalizes unsafe markup usage.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Migrate static HTML to Streamlit native components. Where raw HTML essential, use helper that HTML-escapes inputs.

vulnerabilities.description: No Content-Security-Policy or security headers. Streamlit doesn't support CSP natively; must configure at reverse-proxy level.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Deploy behind nginx/Caddy with CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options headers.

vulnerabilities.description: No rate limiting on any endpoint or pipeline operation. Attacker could trigger repeated expensive pipeline runs, exhausting API rate limits and incurring costs.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add rate limiting at reverse-proxy level or application level. Throttle pipeline runs.

sql_injection_assessment.finding: PASS — No SQL injection vectors detected
sql_injection_assessment.details: All 25+ raw text() SQL queries use parameterized bind params (:sid, :now, :ids). SQLAlchemy ORM usage is safe. One dynamic SQL construction at ingestion/pipeline.py:334 uses OR-join on hardcoded fragments — no user input, but worth monitoring.

recommendations: IMMEDIATE: Rotate DeepSeek API key (sk-96c8c56a71f64942bf5c6630bd8a9924) — in git history + 11 report dumps.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate Google API key (AIzaSyBWRCPmYpHwI_yF3MHWMCrmMe2A92-QkbU) — in git history + 11 report dumps.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate Alpha Vantage API key (FQZDH795B6P1JXSU) — in git history + 11 report dumps.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate Alpaca trading credentials from external/TradingAgents/.env — trading keys exposed on disk.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate database password (stockdb123 in config.yaml history) and restrict MySQL to localhost only.
IMMEDIATE: Add 'reports/' to .gitignore. Delete all effective_config.yaml files with secrets from reports/full_pipeline/.
CRITICAL: Move ALL secrets to environment variables or .env + python-dotenv. Use config.yaml.example as committed template.
CRITICAL: Purge git history of secrets using BFG Repo-Cleaner or rotate all keys and force-push rewritten history.
HIGH: Replace joblib.load() with ONNX/safetensors. If joblib required, validate model with SHA-256 checksum before loading.
HIGH: Add authentication to Streamlit (streamlit-authenticator for quick fix, OAuth2 proxy for production).
HIGH: Stop writing API secrets to os.environ globally. Use explicit argument passing or temporary context manager.
MEDIUM: Replace unsafe_allow_html f-string patterns with st.metric() and safe Streamlit components.
MEDIUM: Redact API keys from effective_config.yaml dumps — mask values as '***REDACTED***'.
MEDIUM: Add bandit to CI/CD: bandit -r src/ -f json.
LOW: Deploy behind reverse proxy with CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options.
LOW: Add rate limiting to pipeline endpoints and expensive operations.
LOW: Audit all 13 unsafe_allow_html sites and convert to safe rendering.

prior_audit_comparison.delta: No vulnerabilities fixed since v1. Risk score unchanged at 86/100.
new_findings: reports/ directory leak confirmed — 11 effective_config.yaml files with live keys
XSS f-string analysis deepened — stock name/industry from DB is stored XSS vector
os.environ injection quantified — 3 injection points across backends + ingestion
